# Comprehensive Documentation Update - Sync Docs with Reality

**Status:** Planning

## Overview

Synchronize all project documentation to accurately reflect the current state of the codebase. Major features shipped in November 2025 - January 2026 (AI Terminal, 40+ Dashboard Widgets, Notes Full Implementation, Calendar Phase 3) are not reflected in planning documents.

## Problem Statement

**Documentation Drift Severity: CRITICAL**

The agent's comprehensive analysis revealed:

1. **Massive Undocumented Progress:** Project is ~15-20% complete (not 1% as docs suggest)
2. **Feature Map Out of Date:** Shows features as "❌ Not shipped" when they're production-ready
3. **Roadmap Timeline Wrong:** Says "Q1-Q4 2026" for features shipped Nov 2025 - Jan 2026
4. **Major Features Missing:** AI Terminal (8 providers) not mentioned in feature_map.md or roadmap

6. **Duplicate Files:** Design docs duplicated between docs/technical/ and docs/brand/

- New developers will be confused about project status
- Feature planning based on outdated roadmap
- Missing credit for major achievements (AI Terminal, Dashboard)
- Broken references prevent using automation tools

### Phase 1-2 (Weeks 1-6): Critical Mass
**Goal:** Fix embarrassing bugs, make Notes production-ready
- **Target:** Notes at 70%, Dashboard at 60%
- **Result:** App becomes credible for note-taking use case

### Phase 3-4 (Weeks 7-12): Expand Capabilities
**Goal:** Calendar and Kanban to professional-grade
- **Target:** Calendar at 65%, Kanban at 70%
- **Result:** App becomes all-in-one productivity suite

### Phase 5-6 (Weeks 13-16): Polish & Integrate
**Goal:** Dashboard as hub, proper settings, imports
- **Target:** Dashboard at 75%, Settings at 70%
- **Result:** App feels cohesive and professional

### Phase 7-8 (Weeks 17-20): Integration & Quality
**Goal:** Features work together, production-ready quality
- **Target:** All features at 75-80%
- **Result:** Ship v1.0, ready for marketing push

## Implementation Phases

### 📋 Phase 1: Critical Bug Fixes & Quick Wins (Week 1-2)
**File:** `tasks/phase-1-critical-fixes.md`

**Focus:** Fix embarrassing UX bugs, unlock existing functionality

**Notes Quick Wins:**
- Fix toolbar buttons (Bold, Italic, Underline)
- Add keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+B, Cmd+I, etc.)
- Implement word/character count
- Build search UI (function exists, needs interface)
- Enable folder nesting (data model ready)

**Calendar Quick Wins:**
- Add time picker to events
- Add event categories/colors
- Show event time in cells

**Kanban Quick Wins:**
- Display tags on cards
- Show due date indicators
- Add estimated hours badges

**Dashboard Quick Wins:**
- Task Summary widget
- Upcoming Events widget
- Recent Notes widget
- Quick Add panel

**Settings Quick Wins:**
- Rename to "Backup & Restore"
- Create separate Settings page
- Add keyboard shortcuts reference

**Impact:** Users see immediate improvements, critical bugs fixed
**Estimated:** 2 weeks, ~40-60 hours

---

### 📝 Phase 2: Notes to Production-Ready (Week 3-6)
**File:** `tasks/phase-2-notes-production-ready.md`

**Goal:** Match Obsidian 70%, Notion 60%

**Rich Text Features:**
- Images/attachments (drag-drop, paste)
- Tables (Lexical TablePlugin)
- Checklists/todo items
- Code syntax highlighting
- Slash commands (`/heading`, `/table`)
- Markdown shortcuts (`**bold**` → bold)

**Organization:**
- Nested folders (tree view, drag-drop)
- Note templates
- Favorites/starred notes
- Wiki-style linking `[[Note]]`
- Tags autocomplete

**Search & Discovery:**
- Full-text search UI
- Search filters (folder, tags, date)
- Search highlighting
- Quick switcher (Cmd+K)

**Export:**
- Export to Markdown/PDF
- Bulk export folders
- Print notes

**Impact:** Notes becomes credible Second Brain tool
**Estimated:** 4 weeks, ~80-120 hours

---

### 📅 Phase 3: Calendar to Professional-Grade (Week 7-9)
**File:** `tasks/phase-3-calendar-professional-grade.md`

**Goal:** Match Google Calendar 60-70%

**Time Management:**
- Day view (hour-by-hour)
- Time slots in week view
- Drag-drop events
- Resize events (change duration)

**Event Features:**
- Recurring events (daily/weekly/monthly)
- Multi-day events
- Event reminders (browser notifications)
- Event location
- Event attachments

**Advanced:**
- Import/export .ics files
- Conflict detection
- Availability view
- Agenda view (list format)

**Impact:** Calendar becomes serious scheduling tool
**Estimated:** 3 weeks, ~60-80 hours

---

### 📊 Phase 4: Kanban to PM Powerhouse (Week 10-12)
**File:** `tasks/phase-4-kanban-pm-powerhouse.md`

**Goal:** Match Linear 70%, Jira 60%

**Customization:**
- Custom columns (add/remove/rename)
- Swimlanes (group by priority/assignee)
- Column limits (WIP constraints)
- Board templates

**Card Features:**
- Card detail panel (full-screen view)
- Subtasks/checklist
- Comments/discussion
- Activity log
- Card cover images

**Advanced PM:**
- Dependencies (blocking/blocked by)
- Time tracking UI
- Progress bars (0-100%)
- Labels/custom fields
- Bulk operations

**Views:**
- List view (table)
- Timeline view (Gantt)
- Card numbers (TASK-001)

**Impact:** Kanban becomes serious PM tool
**Estimated:** 3 weeks, ~60-80 hours

---

### 🎛️ Phase 5: Dashboard as Command Center (Week 13-14)
**File:** `tasks/phase-5-dashboard-command-center.md`

**Goal:** Match Notion Dashboard 70%

**Customization:**
- Drag-drop widget layout
- Resize widgets
- Add/remove widgets
- Save layout preferences

**Widgets:**
- Task summary (status, priority, due)
- Calendar preview (today, week)
- Recent notes
- Time tracking summary
- Habit tracker
- Focus timer (Pomodoro)
- Quick stats

**Personalization:**
- Greeting (Good morning, [Name])
- Custom background
- Welcome checklist for new users

**Impact:** Dashboard becomes central productivity hub
**Estimated:** 2 weeks, ~30-40 hours

---

### ⚙️ Phase 6: Settings & Integrations (Week 15-16)
**File:** `tasks/phase-6-settings-integrations.md`

**Goal:** Professional app settings, data portability

**App Settings:**
- Theme customization
- Language selection (i18n prep)
- Date/time format
- Timezone
- Startup page
- Display density

**Keyboard Shortcuts:**
- Global shortcuts reference
- Customizable shortcuts
- Conflict detection

**Import/Export:**
- Import from Notion (HTML/Markdown)
- Import from Obsidian (Markdown)
- Import from CSV
- Export all data (JSON, Markdown)

**Advanced:**
- Storage management
- Clear feature data
- Developer mode
- Experimental features toggle

**Impact:** App feels professional and configurable
**Estimated:** 2 weeks, ~30-40 hours

---

### 🔗 Phase 7: Cross-Feature Integration (Week 17-18)
**File:** `tasks/phase-7-cross-feature-integration.md`

**Goal:** Features work together seamlessly

**Integrations:**
- Link notes to tasks/events (`[[Note]]`)
- Convert note text to tasks
- Embed calendar in notes
- Task deadlines sync to calendar
- Daily notes auto-create

**Automation:**
- Auto-tag notes by keywords
- Auto-move completed tasks
- Auto-archive old tasks
- Template automation (weekly review)

**Unified Search:**
- Global search (notes, tasks, events)
- Search from dashboard
- Recent items (unified history)

**Impact:** App becomes cohesive ecosystem
**Estimated:** 2 weeks, ~30-40 hours

---

### ✨ Phase 8: Polish & Performance (Week 19-20)
**File:** `tasks/phase-8-polish-performance.md`

**Goal:** Production-ready quality

**Performance:**
- Lazy loading for large notes
- Virtual scrolling
- Debounce search/filters
- Optimize re-renders
- Code splitting
- Bundle size < 100KB

**Design Polish:**
- Consistent spacing
- Loading states (skeletons)
- Empty states (helpful guidance)
- Error states
- Smooth animations
- Mobile responsive

**Accessibility:**
- ARIA labels
- Keyboard navigation
- Focus management
- Screen reader support
- High contrast mode

**Testing:**
- Unit tests (critical functions)
- Integration tests (user flows)
- E2E tests (full scenarios)
- Performance benchmarks

**Impact:** App ready for v1.0 launch
**Estimated:** 2 weeks, ~40-60 hours

---

## Total Timeline & Resources

**Duration:** 20 weeks (~5 months)
**Total Effort:** 400-600 hours of development
**Sprints:** 10 x 2-week sprints
**Pace:** ~20-30 hours/week average

## Success Metrics

### Week 6 (After Phase 1-2):
- ✅ Notes: 70% feature parity (production-ready)
- ✅ Dashboard: 60% feature parity (useful)
- ✅ All critical bugs fixed
- ✅ App credible for note-taking

### Week 12 (After Phase 1-4):
- ✅ Notes: 75%
- ✅ Calendar: 65%
- ✅ Kanban: 70%
- ✅ Dashboard: 60%
- ✅ App credible for all-in-one productivity

### Week 16 (After Phase 1-6):
- ✅ Notes: 80%
- ✅ Calendar: 70%
- ✅ Kanban: 75%
- ✅ Dashboard: 75%
- ✅ Settings: 70%
- ✅ App feels professional

### Week 20 (After All Phases):
- ✅ All features: 75-80%+ parity
- ✅ Features integrated seamlessly
- ✅ Production-ready quality
- ✅ Ready for v1.0 launch

## Competitive Positioning

### Current (Pre-improvements):
- **Privacy-first:** ✅ Unique
- **Local-first:** ✅ Unique
- **Feature completeness:** ❌ 30-40% parity
- **Position:** "Interesting MVP, not production-ready"

### After Phase 1-2 (Week 6):
- **Privacy-first:** ✅ Unique
- **Local-first:** ✅ Unique
- **Notes:** ✅ Obsidian competitor (70%)
- **Position:** "Best privacy-first note-taking app"

### After Phase 1-4 (Week 12):
- **Privacy-first:** ✅ Unique
- **Local-first:** ✅ Unique
- **All features:** ✅ 65-75% parity
- **Position:** "Privacy-first Notion alternative"

### After All Phases (Week 20):
- **Privacy-first:** ✅ Unique
- **Local-first:** ✅ Unique
- **All features:** ✅ 75-80%+ parity
- **Integration:** ✅ Seamless ecosystem
- **Position:** "The privacy-first productivity suite for individuals and small teams"

## Unique Value Proposition

**After completion:**

1. **Privacy-First (No competitors):**
   - 100% local-first, zero cloud dependency
   - Zero tracking, zero analytics
   - User owns their data completely

2. **All-in-One (Few competitors):**
   - Notes + Tasks + Calendar + Time Tracking in one app
   - Seamless integration (link notes to tasks, etc.)
   - Single .brain file backup

3. **Free Forever (Freemium model):**
   - Core features 100% free forever
   - No feature paywalls on privacy features
   - No storage limits (IndexedDB 50GB+)

4. **Beautiful UX:**
   - Clean, modern design
   - Fast performance (< 100KB bundle)
   - Accessible (WCAG AA)

5. **Future-Ready:**
   - AI-powered features (future)
   - Plugin system (future)
   - E2EE backups (Q2 2026)

**Market Position:** "The privacy-first alternative to Notion for individuals who value data ownership"

## Dependencies & Risks

### Technical Dependencies:
- ✅ Lexical editor (already integrated)
- ✅ IndexedDB (already integrated)
- ✅ @dnd-kit (already integrated for Kanban)
- ⚠️ New: react-grid-layout (for Dashboard customization)
- ⚠️ New: date-fns/dayjs (for better date handling in Calendar)
- ⚠️ New: rrule (for recurring events)

### Risks:

**1. Scope Creep:**
- **Risk:** 20 weeks becomes 30+ weeks
- **Mitigation:** Strict sprint boundaries, ship incrementally

**2. Feature Complexity:**
- **Risk:** Some features (Gantt chart, recurring events) harder than estimated
- **Mitigation:** Research phase before each sprint, adjust if needed

**3. Performance:**
- **Risk:** Bundle size grows beyond 100KB
- **Mitigation:** Monitor bundle size each sprint, aggressive code splitting

**4. User Feedback:**
- **Risk:** Priorities shift based on user feedback
- **Mitigation:** Flexibility to adjust phases, maintain 80/20 rule

**5. Dependency Issues:**
- **Risk:** New libraries introduce bugs/vulnerabilities
- **Mitigation:** Thoroughly vet dependencies, prefer battle-tested libraries

## Next Steps

1. ✅ Master plan created (this file)
2. ⏳ Create Phase 1 detailed plan: `tasks/phase-1-critical-fixes.md`

# Fix Kanban Card Delete → Detail Panel Bug

**Session ID:** 011CWKtxzgM5qHHf9DhdBYy6

**Status:** 🎯 PLANNING
**Estimated Time:** 30 minutes

---

## Overview

Fix bug where deleting a task from the card menu causes the detail panel to open after deletion is confirmed.

---

## Problem Statement

### Current Behavior (Bug)

1. User clicks "⋮" menu on a Kanban card
2. User clicks "Delete" in the dropdown menu
3. Confirmation dialog appears
4. User confirms deletion
5. Task is deleted ✅
6. **BUG:** Card detail panel opens even though the task no longer exists ❌

### Root Cause Analysis

The card component has a click handler on the entire card div that opens the detail panel. When clicking the delete button in the menu, we added `e.stopPropagation()` to prevent the immediate panel opening. However, after the delete confirmation dialog closes, something is triggering the card's onClick handler.

**Likely Issue:** The menu button's stopPropagation prevents the immediate click, but when the dialog closes, React might be re-rendering or the click event is bubbling up after the dialog unmounts.

### Desired Behavior

1. User clicks "⋮" menu on a Kanban card
2. User clicks "Delete" in the dropdown menu
3. Confirmation dialog appears
4. User confirms deletion
5. Task is deleted ✅
6. No detail panel opens ✅

---

## Files to Read

- [x] `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanCard.tsx` - Card component with menu and delete logic
- [x] `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanColumn.tsx` - Column component that may be triggering card click
- [x] `src/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx` - Delete confirmation dialog

---

## Root Cause Investigation

### KanbanCard.tsx Structure

The card has multiple click handlers:
1. **Card div** (lines 165-177): Opens detail panel via `onCardClick` prop
2. **Menu button** (lines 183-191): Opens menu, has stopPropagation ✅
3. **Edit button** (lines 194-203): Opens edit mode, has stopPropagation ✅
4. **Delete button** (lines 204-213): Opens delete dialog, has stopPropagation ✅

### Current Delete Flow

```typescript
// Line 77-80
const handleDelete = useCallback(() => {
  setShowDeleteDialog(true);
  setShowMenu(false);
}, []);

// Line 82-85
const confirmDelete = useCallback(() => {
  deleteTask(task.id);
  setShowDeleteDialog(false);
}, [task.id, deleteTask]);
```

**Problem:** After `confirmDelete()` executes and the dialog closes, the card component is still mounted briefly before React removes it from the DOM. During this brief moment, a click event might be propagating to the card's onClick handler.

---

## Proposed Solution

### Option A: Flag to Prevent Click After Delete (RECOMMENDED)

Add a local state flag `isDeleting` that prevents the card click handler from firing:

```typescript
const [isDeleting, setIsDeleting] = useState(false);

const handleDelete = useCallback(() => {
  setIsDeleting(true); // Prevent future clicks
  setShowDeleteDialog(true);
  setShowMenu(false);
}, []);

const confirmDelete = useCallback(() => {
  deleteTask(task.id);
  setShowDeleteDialog(false);
}, [task.id, deleteTask]);

const handleCardClick = useCallback(() => {
  if (!isDeleting) {
    onCardClick(task);
  }
}, [isDeleting, task, onCardClick]);

// Update card div onClick
<div onClick={handleCardClick}>
```

**Pros:**
- Simple, surgical fix
- Prevents any click events after delete is initiated
- No side effects

**Cons:**
- Adds one extra state variable

### Option B: Remove Card Click Handler During Delete

Conditionally render the card without the onClick handler when deleting:

```typescript
const cardProps = isDeleting ? {} : {
  onClick: () => onCardClick(task)
};

<div {...cardProps}>
```

**Pros:**
- Cleaner - no handler means no possibility of triggering

**Cons:**
- More complex conditional logic

### Decision: Option A

Option A is cleaner and more explicit. It clearly signals "this card is being deleted, don't respond to clicks."

---

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Add `isDeleting` State (5 min)

**File:** `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanCard.tsx`

Add state:
```typescript
const [isDeleting, setIsDeleting] = useState(false);
```

### Step 2: Set Flag on Delete (5 min)

Update `handleDelete`:
```typescript
const handleDelete = useCallback(() => {
  setIsDeleting(true);
  setShowDeleteDialog(true);
  setShowMenu(false);
}, []);
```

### Step 3: Extract Card Click Handler (10 min)

Currently, the card div has an inline arrow function:
```typescript
<div onClick={() => onCardClick(task)}>
```

Extract to named function with guard:
```typescript
const handleCardClick = useCallback(() => {
  if (!isDeleting && onCardClick) {
    onCardClick(task);
  }
}, [isDeleting, task, onCardClick]);

// Update card div
<div onClick={handleCardClick}>
```

### Step 4: Handle Cancel Case (5 min)

Reset flag if user cancels delete:
```typescript
const cancelDelete = useCallback(() => {
  setShowDeleteDialog(false);
  setIsDeleting(false); // Reset flag

**Created:** 2025-01-22
**Status:** In Progress
**Priority:** P0 (Architecture Foundation)
**Complexity:** Medium
**Estimated Effort:** 2-3 weeks
**New Page Name:** "Time & Planning"

---

## Context

Phase 3 (Time Tracking) is complete with 71% Toggl parity. Before implementing Phase 4 (Billing Features), we need to consolidate the duplicate calendar systems.

**User Direction:**
- TimeEntryCalendar looks WAY better than Planning Calendar Widget
- Add Planning features (events, recurring, multi-day) to TimeEntryCalendar
- Replace Planning page entirely with enhanced Time Tracking
- Rename to **"Time & Planning"** to show dual purpose

**Strategy:** Extend TimeEntryCalendar with event functionality, delete old Planning Calendar Widget.

---

## Scope

### Phase 3.7A: Enhance TimeEntryCalendar with Events (Week 1-2)
**Goal:** Add event creation, recurring events, multi-day support to beautiful TimeEntryCalendar

### Phase 3.7B: Replace Planning Page (Week 2-3)
**Goal:** Rename Time Tracking → "Time & Planning", delete old Planning page

### Phase 3.7C: Cleanup & Optimization (Week 3)
**Goal:** Remove duplicate Calendar Widget code, optimize bundle size

---

## Architecture Plan (SIMPLIFIED)

### Enhanced TimeEntryCalendar

**Keep:** Beautiful TimeEntryCalendar as foundation (src/components/TimeEntryCalendar.tsx)

**Add:**
```
src/components/
├── TimeEntryCalendar.tsx        # ENHANCE with event support
├── EventCreateModal.tsx         # NEW - Create/edit events
├── RecurringEventModal.tsx      # NEW - Configure recurrence
└── EventBlock.tsx               # NEW - Render event blocks
```

**Strategy:** Extend existing TimeEntryCalendar to render BOTH time entries AND events
- Time entries: Project-colored blocks (existing)
- Events: Magenta/blue bars (NEW)
- Visual distinction clear
- Unified navigation (existing)
- Click day → choose "Add Event" or "Add Time Entry"

### Data Adapter Pattern

```typescript
interface CalendarItem {
  id: string;
  date: Date;
  title: string;
  color?: string;
  timeRange?: { start: string; end: string };
  type: 'event' | 'time-entry' | 'task';
  metadata: any; // Original item reference
}

interface CalendarDataAdapter<T> {
  adapt(items: T[]): CalendarItem[];
  getColor(item: T): string;
  getTimeRange(item: T): { start: string; end: string } | null;
  onClick(item: T): void;
}
```

### DateTime Module Structure

```
src/utils/datetime/
├── index.ts              # Public API exports
├── formatting.ts         # formatDate, formatTime, formatDuration
├── duration.ts           # Duration calculations and parsing
├── arithmetic.ts         # Date math (add/subtract days, months)
└── relative.ts           # Relative time formatting ("2 hours ago")
```

### Search Utility Structure

```typescript
// src/utils/search.ts

interface SearchOptions {
  caseSensitive?: boolean;
  fuzzy?: boolean;
  threshold?: number; // Fuzzy match threshold (0-1)
}

function createSearchMatcher(
  query: string,
  options?: SearchOptions
): (text: string) => boolean

function searchMultipleFields<T>(
  items: T[],
  fields: (keyof T)[],
  query: string,
  options?: SearchOptions
): T[]

function filterByTags<T extends { tags: string[] }>(
  items: T[],
  tags: string[]
): T[]

function highlightMatches(
  text: string,
  query: string
): string // Returns HTML with <mark> tags
```

## Technical Details

### Calendar Item Adapters

**Event Adapter:**
```typescript
class EventAdapter implements CalendarDataAdapter<CalendarEvent> {
  adapt(events: CalendarEvent[]): CalendarItem[] {
    return events.map(event => ({
      id: event.id,
      date: new Date(event.date),
      title: event.title,
      color: 'magenta', // Default event color
      timeRange: event.isAllDay ? null : {
        start: event.startTime,
        end: event.endTime
      },
      type: 'event',
      metadata: event
    }));
  }

  getColor(event: CalendarEvent): string {
    return 'magenta'; // Or event.category color
  }

  onClick(event: CalendarEvent): void {
    // Open event edit modal
  }
}
```

**Time Entry Adapter:**
```typescript
class TimeEntryAdapter implements CalendarDataAdapter<TimeEntry> {
  constructor(private projects: Map<string, Project>) {}

  adapt(entries: TimeEntry[]): CalendarItem[] {
    return entries.map(entry => {
      const project = this.projects.get(entry.projectId);
      return {
        id: entry.id,
        date: new Date(entry.startTime),
        title: entry.description,
        color: project?.color,
        timeRange: {
          start: formatTime(new Date(entry.startTime)),
          end: entry.endTime ? formatTime(new Date(entry.endTime)) : 'Running'
        },
        type: 'time-entry',
        metadata: entry
      };
    });
  }

  getColor(entry: TimeEntry): string {
    const project = this.projects.get(entry.projectId);
    return project?.color || '#6B7280'; // Gray fallback
  }

  onClick(entry: TimeEntry): void {
    // Open EditTimeEntryModal
  }
}
```

### DateTime Migration Example

**After:**

import { formatDuration, formatDate, isToday } from '../utils/datetime';
```

### Search Migration Example

**Before (in useNotesStore):**
```typescript
searchNotes: (query) => {
  return get().notes.filter(note =>
    note.title.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()) ||
    note.content.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()) ||
    note.tags.some(tag => tag.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()))
  );
}
```

**After:**
```typescript
import { searchMultipleFields, filterByTags } from '../utils/search';

searchNotes: (query) => {

  return searchMultipleFields(notes, ['title', 'content'], query);
}
```

# Task: Fix Footer Pill Alignment & Mobile Research

**Created:** 2025-11-23
**Status:** In Progress
**Priority:** P2

## Objectives
- [x] Fix desktop alignment (currently pushed right)
  - [x] Update `Footer.tsx` to use Framer Motion `x` prop instead of `style` transform to avoid conflicts.
- [ ] Research mobile footer improvements
  - [ ] Analyze current mobile footprint.
  - [ ] Propose 2-3 alternative designs for mobile (e.g., icon-only, stacked, or integrated into nav).

## Context
User reports the footer pill is pushed to the right on desktop. This is likely due to a conflict between CSS `transform` in `style` prop and Framer Motion's animation of `y`, which overwrites the transform string.
For mobile, the pill is too large (width, height, font size).

## Implementation Plan
1. Modify `src/components/Footer.tsx` to move `translateX(-50%)` into `initial` and `animate` props.
2. Verify the fix visually (if possible) or by code logic.
3. Document mobile research findings in `tasks/fix-footer-pill.md` or a separate note.

## Mobile Research Findings

### Problem Analysis
The current footer uses `px-6 py-3` (24px horizontal, 12px vertical padding) and `gap-4` (16px gap). On mobile, this creates a large visual footprint that obscures content.

### Proposed Solutions

#### Option 1: The "Micro-Pill" (Recommended)
Drastically reduce padding and gaps for screens `< 640px`.
- **Padding**: `px-3 py-1.5` (vs `px-6 py-3`)
- **Font**: `text-[10px]` (vs `text-xs`)
- **Content**: Abbreviate text.
    - "Privacy Policy" -> "Privacy"
    - "About Us" -> "About"
    - "© 2025 Neuman Brain" -> "© 2025"

#### Option 2: The "Glass Bar"
Instead of a floating pill, use a full-width thin bar at the bottom.
- **Height**: Fixed small height (e.g., 24px).
- **Position**: `bottom-0` (no floating margin).
- **Pros**: Doesn't float over content, feels more like system UI.
- **Cons**: Less "modern/dynamic" than the pill.

#### Option 3: "Icon Only"
Collapse everything into a single "Info" icon or a small set of icons.
- **Default**: Just a small `(i)` icon or copyright symbol in the corner.
- **Interaction**: Tap to expand to full footer.
- **Pros**: Minimal footprint.
- **Cons**: Low discoverability for links.

### Recommendation
Implement **Option 1 (Micro-Pill)**. It maintains the design language but scales it down appropriately for the device.
- Add `sm:` prefixes to current padding/gap classes.
- Add smaller base classes for mobile.

   - **Status:** Separate task needed

### Priority 1: Multi-Day Event Bars Implementation
**From:** `fix-calendar-ux-issues.md` (deferred work)

**Goal:** Render multi-day events as continuous colored bars spanning days (like Google Calendar)

**Current State:**
- Multi-day events show text with arrows: `← Event Title →`
- Hard to visually see which days an event spans

**Desired State:**
- Continuous colored bar behind event text
- Bar spans multiple day columns
- Rounded edges on start/end days

**Implementation Options:**
1. **Option A:** CSS Grid with `grid-column: span N`
   - Requires restructuring day cells to allow interleaved event bars
   - Cleaner approach but more refactoring

2. **Option B:** Absolute Positioning
   - Calculate bar position based on start/end columns
   - Less refactoring but more complex calculation
   - May have alignment issues

# Widget System Refinements - UI Polish & Bug Fixes

**Status:** 🎯 PLANNING
**Priority:** P2 - MEDIUM
**Created:** 2025-11-24 (Consolidated from 2 related tasks)

---

## Overview

Consolidated plan for widget system UI polish, bug fixes, and refinements.

---

## Consolidated From

This task consolidates the following 2 related task files:
1. `widget-ui-fixes.md` - Widget UI bug fixes
2. `widget-ui-refinements.md` - Widget UI polish and improvements

**Reason for Consolidation:** Both tasks focus on widget UI polish and refinements with similar scope.

---

## Objectives

### Phase 1: Bug Fixes
- Fix widget rendering edge cases
- Resolve widget control visibility issues
- Fix widget state persistence bugs
- Ensure cross-browser compatibility

### Phase 2: UI Polish
- Improve widget animations and transitions
- Enhance widget loading states
- Polish widget hover/focus states
- Standardize widget spacing and padding

### Phase 3: Refinements
- Optimize widget performance
- Improve widget accessibility
- Enhance widget error handling
- Add widget customization options

---

## Known Issues to Fix

### High Priority
1. **Widget Controls Overlap** - Controls overlap content on narrow widgets
2. **Loading State Flicker** - Widgets flicker during lazy load
3. **Drag Handle Visibility** - Not clear when widget is draggable
4. **Error State Handling** - Widget errors crash entire dashboard

### Medium Priority
1. **Animation Performance** - Some widget transitions janky on mobile
2. **Focus States** - Keyboard navigation not obvious
3. **Dark Mode Contrast** - Some widgets have low contrast in dark mode
4. **Mobile Responsiveness** - Widget controls too small on mobile

### Low Priority
1. **Widget Skeleton** - Loading skeleton doesn't match widget content
2. **Tooltip Positioning** - Tooltips cut off at screen edges
3. **Icon Consistency** - Some widgets use different icon styles
4. **Padding Inconsistency** - Widget internal padding varies

---

## UI Refinements

### Visual Improvements
1. **Consistent Shadows**
   - Standardize widget shadow depth
   - Use semantic shadow tokens
   - Ensure shadows work in light/dark mode

2. **Improved Borders**
   - Use consistent border radius (8px rounded-button)
   - Standardize border colors (semantic tokens)
   - Add subtle borders for better widget definition

3. **Better Spacing**
   - Consistent internal padding (16px/24px)
   - Standardize gap between widget elements
   - Improve header/content/footer spacing

4. **Enhanced Interactions**
   - Smooth hover states (200ms transitions)
   - Clear focus indicators (accent-blue ring)
   - Better drag-drop feedback

---

## Implementation Plan

### Week 1: Critical Bug Fixes
- [ ] Fix widget controls overlap
- [ ] Fix loading state flicker
- [ ] Improve drag handle visibility
- [ ] Add error boundaries to widgets

### Week 2: UI Polish
- [ ] Standardize widget shadows
- [ ] Improve widget borders
- [ ] Fix spacing inconsistencies
- [ ] Polish animations and transitions

### Week 3: Refinements
- [ ] Improve widget accessibility (ARIA labels, keyboard nav)
- [ ] Optimize widget performance (lazy loading, bundle splitting)
- [ ] Enhance error handling (fallback UI, error messages)
- [ ] Add widget customization (themes, layouts)

### Week 4: Testing & Documentation
- [ ] Cross-browser testing (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
- [ ] Mobile device testing (iOS, Android)
- [ ] Accessibility audit (WCAG AA compliance)
- [ ] Update widget documentation

---

## Success Criteria

### Bug Fixes Complete When:
- ✅ Zero widget rendering errors
- ✅ Controls don't overlap on any widget size
- ✅ No loading state flicker
- ✅ Drag handles clearly visible

### UI Polish Complete When:
- ✅ Consistent shadows, borders, spacing across all widgets
- ✅ Smooth animations (no jank on 60fps devices)
- ✅ Clear hover/focus states
- ✅ Mobile-friendly controls

### Refinements Complete When:
- ✅ WCAG AA compliant
- ✅ < 1s widget load time
- ✅ Graceful error handling
- ✅ Customization options available

---

## Files to Modify

### Core Widget System
- `src/components/Widget.tsx` - Main widget wrapper
- `src/components/WidgetControls.tsx` - Widget controls component
- `src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` - Dashboard layout
- `src/index.css` - Widget styles

### Individual Widgets
- `src/widgets/Dashboard/*.tsx` - All dashboard widgets (as needed)

---

## Design System Alignment

### Semantic Tokens to Use
- **Backgrounds:** `bg-surface-light`, `bg-surface-dark`, `bg-surface-light-elevated`
- **Borders:** `border-border-light`, `border-border-dark`
- **Text:** `text-text-light-primary`, `text-text-dark-primary`
- **Shadows:** `shadow-card`, `shadow-elevated`
- **Spacing:** 4px base unit (0.25rem multiples)

### Component Standards
- **Border Radius:** `rounded-button` (8px)
- **Transitions:** `transition-all duration-standard ease-smooth` (200ms)
- **Focus Ring:** `ring-2 ring-accent-blue`
- **Hover States:** Subtle background change, smooth transition

---

## Testing Checklist

### Visual Testing
- [ ] Light mode - all widgets
- [ ] Dark mode - all widgets
- [ ] Mobile (< 640px) - all widgets
- [ ] Tablet (640-1024px) - all widgets
- [ ] Desktop (> 1024px) - all widgets

### Functional Testing
- [ ] Widget loading (lazy load)
- [ ] Widget controls (resize, remove, settings)
- [ ] Drag-drop reordering
- [ ] Error states and recovery
- [ ] State persistence

### Accessibility Testing
- [ ] Keyboard navigation
- [ ] Screen reader compatibility
- [ ] Focus indicators
- [ ] ARIA labels
- [ ] Color contrast (WCAG AA)

---

## Notes

**Why Consolidate?**
- Both tasks focus on widget UI improvements
- Overlapping scope (bugs vs polish)
- Easier to coordinate changes
- Single source of truth for widget refinements

**Priority Order:**
1. Bug fixes first (blocking issues)
2. UI polish second (user-facing improvements)
3. Refinements third (nice-to-have enhancements)

**Time Estimates:**
- Bug fixes: 1 week
- UI polish: 1 week
- Refinements: 1 week
- Testing: 1 week
- **Total:** 4 weeks to complete all work

---

**Document Status:** Active Planning
**Last Updated:** 2025-11-24
**Next Review:** After completing bug fixes
